The choice of groups of "comparable" proviral load is, to some extent, subjective but a range of alternative groupings gave similar results. This
included a grouping in which the mean proviral load of ACs was higher than the mean proviral load of HAM/TSP patients in each group (it was
necessary to omit some high proviral load HAM/TSP patients in order to obtain this alternative grouping). We have illustrated our results using this
particular grouping because it is a representative grouping and because it yields two or more subjects in each group thus minimising the effect of
outliers.
